subject: Rolycoly, a dark gray coal-rock creature shaped like a gear or wheel with a single glowing orange eye
description: A dark gray creature composed of layered angular coal and rock segments, roughly circular and gear-like in shape, rests on a rocky surface inside what appears to be a cave or mine. It has a single large glowing orange eye at its center, which provides the only warm light in an otherwise cool-toned scene. The cave walls behind it are dark with hints of blue-teal, suggesting moisture or mineral deposits. The creature's rocky body is rendered with textured angular facets suggesting coal seams or geological strata. The setting feels underground and enclosed.
foreground: dark angular coal-rock gear-shaped creature on rocky surface
background: cave walls with cool teal and dark tones, rocky cavern interior
emotion: still and watchful, single eye glowing in dark cave
